data.title: NFTs gaining traction: Nightclubs, rappers rejoin trend. DappRadar's Sara Gherghelas notes increasing activity.
data.description: According to DappRadar analyst Sara Gherghelas, recent data indicates a noticeable resurgence in interest within the NFT sector.

data.title: Yutong Bus unveils next-gen IoV platform Link+ for fleets, boosting efficiency. Stock: Yutong (SHA: 600066).
data.description: In Zhengzhou, China, on September 4, 2025, Yutong Bus (SHA: 600066), a prominent electric bus manufacturer, introduced the latest iteration of its Internet of Vehicles (IoV) platform, Link+. This international version, a significant enhancement of Vehicle+, provides a variety of features aimed at revolutionizing the connectivity in transport.

data.title: Micron commits to investing in AI education to boost opportunities for American youth. #AI #education #investment
data.description: Last month, a total of 500 new COVID-19 cases were reported in the city, marking a 25% increase from the previous month. The number of active cases currently stands at 200, with 50 patients in critical condition. Additionally, 10 new virus-related deaths were recorded, bringing the total death toll in the city to 100. The local health authorities are working to control the spread of the virus through increased testing and vaccination efforts.

data.title: Healthpeak Properties releases 14th annual Corporate Impact Report emphasizing sustainability, governance, and innovation.
data.description: Healthpeak Properties, Inc. (NYSE: DOC) revealed the launch of its 14th annual 2024 Corporate Impact Report, emphasizing a resilient portfolio and sustainability efforts. The report underlines Healthpeak's commitment to promoting workplace culture through its WE CARE core values and ensuring strong corporate governance with transparency. The company's properties drive innovation in patient care and research, contributing to accelerating scientific progress.

data.title: Samsara's Q2 earnings beat estimates with adjusted 12 cents/share and $391.48 million revenue, stock rises after hours.
data.description: Samsara, Inc. (NYSE: IOT) has revealed its second-quarter financial performance after Thursday's market close. Adjusted earnings stood at 12 cents per share, surpassing the anticipated seven cents. The quarterly revenue amounted to $391.48 million, exceeding the analyst forecast of $372.21 million. Following this report, IOT stock has shown a rise in after-hours trading. data.title: JBT Marel Corp prices $500M in notes, with option of additional $75M, due 2030. Initial purchasers given option to buy more.
data.description: JBT Marel Corporation (NYSE and Nasdaq Iceland: JBTM) from Chicago disclosed the pricing of its private offering, valuing $500 million in total for 0.375 percent convertible senior notes due by 2030. The company provided the initial purchasers with an option to acquire up to an extra $75.0 million worth of notes within a 13-day period, commencing on the initial closing date.

data.title: International media test drives GWM Hi4 technology at Dujiangyan, China. GWM's Hi4 hybrid 4WD system impresses with 4WD performance at 2WD consumption level. GWM WEY G9 and GWM TANK 500 Hi4-T receive positive feedback.
data.description: In Baoding, China, an innovative development in driving technology has been introduced by GWM. The Hi4 intelligent hybrid 4WD system by GWM is revolutionizing the driving experience by providing the performance of a 4WD vehicle while maintaining the fuel efficiency of a 2WD vehicle. Through test drives of the GWM WEY G9 and GWM TANK 500 Hi4-T models in various conditions such as city traffic, mountainous terrains, and wet unpaved tracks, international media outlets have witnessed the exceptional efficiency and performance of the GWM Hi4 system. This new technology is receiving positive reviews for its ability to enhance driving capabilities across different environments.

data.title: Sun's wallet blacklisted after $9 million in WLFI tokens moved; Sun denies selling, says only testing occurred.
data.description: Justin Sun, the founder of Tron (CRYPTO: TRX) and a prominent cryptocurrency billionaire, had a wallet address associated with him blacklisted after $9 million worth of World Liberty Financial (WLFI) tokens were transferred out of the address. The on-chain analytics firm Arkham disclosed this information along with evidence from Etherscan showing that World Liberty Financial had blocked the address from making further token transfers. The address linked to Sun moved 50 million WLFI tokens, amounting to $9.11 million based on current prices, to an undisclosed destination. Following these transactions, Sun clarified on X that the address had conducted "general exchange deposit tests" with small amounts before dispersing the tokens to various addresses, emphasizing that no buying or selling activities had taken place. This incident had prompted the address to be restricted. For more details, you can find the full story on Benzinga.com.
